know, sometimes I<|6.14|> <|6.14|> wish I were religious.<|8.38|> <|8.38|> I wish I could have that certainty.<|11.32|> <|11.32|> It just seems so comforting to never doubt things."<|16.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.04|> She then goes on to say,<|2.12|> <|2.22|> I do not find religion to be comforting<|4.76|> <|4.76|> in the way that I think non-religious people mean <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|2.50|> Six questions haunt us in Mark's crucifixion narrative.<|8.50|> <|10.00|> Two asked of Jesus by Pilate, the nervous, bumbling puppet of Rome.<|16.50|> Three are asked of the crowd by Pilate, that puppet,<|6.00|> <|6.00|> "Do you want me to release him?<|9.50|> <|9.50|> And what do you wish me to do with the man you call the king of the Jews?"<|15.00|> <|15.00|> And then when the crowd surprises him with their selfish answer,<|20.00|> <|20.00|> he asks, "Why? What evil has he done?"<|25.00|> <|25.00|> How convenient, Pilate.<|27.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And one finally asked by Jesus of God, "My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?"<|17.20|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I read, I shuddered, and knew.<|7.70|> <|7.70|> This question, dim and naked and pregnant as it is, is a threshold into Holy Week, our<|15.88|> <|15.88|> collective participation in Christ's passion, death, and resurrection.<|21.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Instead of offering a doctrinally neat, theologically tight, or rhetorically eloquent answer to the question of suffering, death, evil, and sin, God offers us God's own self in the person of Jesus Christ.<|19.18|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I am in this with you, God says from the mistrial and the cross.<|6.12|> <|7.00|> Your suffering is my suffering.<|9.78|> <|10.28|> I am with you in your feelings of betrayal, pain, and despair.<|15.16|> <|15.72|> They are mine too.<|17.76|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.34|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Jesus' helpless, diminished body reveals a God who is not quarantined from doubt and alienation.<|11.00|> <|11.00|> This God is with us. This God is for us in the miry clay.<|17.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> as displaced person in Gaza and Venezuelan refugee,<|7.00|> <|7.00|> as the person crushed by debt,<|11.00|> <|11.00|> as the recovering addict,<|14.00|> <|14.00|> as the single parent making ends meet,<|17.00|> <|17.00|> in short, as the crucified class,<|22.00|> <|22.00|> the crucified person, the crucified people.<|28.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The theologian M. Sean Copeland, preaching on Palm Sunday six years ago, states wisely, quote, Jesus of Nazareth discloses for us a distinctive quality of God.<|13.74|> <|13.74|> And this quality disturbs us.<|18.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.14|> Jesus shows us the vulnerability of God, the willingness of God to suffer for us and to suffer with us.<|8.22|> <|8.30|> And even as we know the end of his story, the image of a suffering God disturbs and unsettles us.<|16.98|> <|17.70|> For if our God so suffers, is so exposed to the brutality and power of this world, what shall become of us?<|27.44|> <|27.94|> End quote.<|28.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> What an impossible question.<|4.48|> <|4.48|> Over the course of this week, especially Maundy Thursday, Good Friday, and Easter Day, the<|15.18|> <|15.18|> stark reality of love, of God's unfailing, relentless love in Jesus Christ, is laid bare.<|25.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> As are our hearts, our disappointments, our joys, our aspirations in our cross-shaped world.<|10.68|> <|12.50|> I read, shuddered, and knew.<|16.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This knowing of which Charles Madge speaks, that marrow deep knowing, that teeth chattering<|11.62|> <|11.62|> knowing, that goose bump inducing knowing, that knowing is love.<|22.36|> <|22.36|> God.<|25.36|> <|25.36|> Love.<|27.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|2.80|><|endoftext|>
